game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Chevaliers de Baphomet 1: DC**: Players control either Nico Collard or George Stobbart in this narrative-driven point-and-click adventure, navigating pre-rendered 2D scenes to solve a Templar conspiracy. The core mechanic involves tapping on environmental hotspots to examine objects, collect inventory items, or initiate dialogue with characters, with an inventory icon allowing players to drag and drop items for combination or use. Progression is linear, with new locations and story segments unlocking only after specific puzzles are solved, requiring players to logically deduce solutions by interacting with the environment and utilizing their inventory. Puzzles primarily involve combining inventory items, using them on specific environmental elements, or selecting correct dialogue choices from branching trees to advance the plot. The game concludes upon successfully completing all narrative objectives and uncovering the full conspiracy, with no traditional win/loss states beyond failing to solve a puzzle.
game_info.description: Uncover a mysterious Templar conspiracy in this point-and-click adventure. Explore richly detailed locations, examine objects, collect items, and solve environmental puzzles. Combine inventory items and interact with hotspots to progress through the story. Your goal is to solve all puzzles across multiple locations and reveal the truth behind the conspiracy.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate between menu options and hotspots
Space: Examine/interact with selected hotspot or use inventory item
Z: Open/close inventory menu
Shift: Combine selected inventory items
Enter: Start game
Esc: Pause/unpause game
R: Restart to main menu

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic game mechanics including navigation, hotspot interaction, and inventory management. Validates that the player can move through menus, examine objects, and collect items without errors.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Navigate through available hotspots in the first location, interact with each one, collect any items that appear, and open the inventory to verify items are stored correctly.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Player successfully interacts with all hotspots in the starting location, collects available items, and inventory properly stores them. No crashes or state inconsistencies occur.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing the complete game solution path from start to finish. Validates that all puzzles can be solved in sequence and the game reaches GAME_OVER_WIN state.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Execute optimal puzzle-solving sequence: collect key items in proper order, combine items when needed, use items on correct hotspots, and progress through all locations until reaching the final objective.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Game transitions through all locations successfully, all puzzles are solved correctly, final conspiracy is revealed, and game enters GAME_OVER_WIN state with success message displayed.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing movement and navigation mechanics across different locations. Validates that location transitions work correctly and player can return to previous areas.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Move between locations by interacting with exit hotspots, verify each location loads properly, test navigation in both forward and backward directions.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: All location transitions function smoothly, no broken connections between areas, player can navigate the full game world without getting stuck.
